name: Huldra
primary_sources: Norwegian folktales of Peter Christen Asbjørnsen and Jørgen Moe

distinctive_features: animal's hairy legs and tail, usually a cow's or a fox's
visual_attributes.texture: smooth skin with rough, bark-like back
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: humanoid form with animal tail and tree-like back

domain_specific_visual.CLOTHING_STYLE: long clothing to disguise inhuman features

domain_specific_functional.ROLE_IN_FOLKLORE: keeper or warden of the forest
domain_specific_functional.INTERACTION_WITH_HUMANS: kind to those who respect her

relationships.description: closely related to other underground dwellers

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Scandinavia, including Norway, Sweden, and Sámi regions
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes the hidden and mysterious aspects of nature
primary_sources: Norwegian folktales of Peter Christen Asbjørnsen and Jørgen Moe
